chapter_title.ar: بَابُ مَا يُسْتَحَبُّ مِنَ الْهَدْيِ وَ مَا يَجُوزُ مِنْهُ وَ مَا لَا يَجُوزُ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 181 – What is recommended from the sacrificial animal, and what is allowed from it, and what is not allowed

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (18/166)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (18/166)
text: الْأُضْحِيَّةِ يُكْسَرُ قَرْنُهَا قَالَ إِذَا كَانَ الْقَرْنُ الدَّاخِلُ صَحِيحاً فَهُوَ يُجْزِئُ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Ibn Abu Umeyr, from Jameel,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> regarding the sacrificial animal whose horn is broken. He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘When it was such that its interior horn was correct, so it would suffice’.
en.sarwar: 13. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from ibn abu ‘Umayr from Jamil who has said the following: “I once asked abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about the animal for offering which has a broken horn. He (the Imam) said, ‘If the horn inside is well, then it is sufficient.’”
